SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JUN-05-2009 - JUDGMENT. (CLERK-BETH WILBER, ROOM 103)

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF CRIM, JOHN DEREK RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT BOYD, BENTON SURRELL $3,500.00 PRINCIPAL, $500.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $4,000.00, *$1750 OF THIS AWARD REPRESENTS STATUTORY PENALTIES FOR DEFENDANT'S BAD FAITH RETENTION OF A SECURITY DEPOSIT PER CIVIL CODE 1950.5. ADDITIONALLY, NO DEDUCTION FROM THE SECURITY DEPOSIT WAS MADE FOR THE "AUTOMATIC DEDUCTION" AS A CLEANING FEE AS SUCH FEE CONSTITUTES A NON-REFUNDABLE PORTION OF THE SECURITY DEPOSIT IN VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE 1950.5(M). STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING AMENDED JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF CRIM, JOHN DEREK RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT BOYD, BENTON SURRELL $3,500.00 PRINCIPAL, $50.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $3,550.00, (AMENDED FOR THE PURPOSE OF CORRECTING FEE AMOUNT FROM $500.00 TO $50.00) STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED

calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Sep-22-2009 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: 10:45am (related cases 829205 & 829206 are heard together)
Cause called for Small Claims Appeal, Judge Pro Tem Richard Beckman, nothing on record, clerk-Beth Wilber, Dept. 525
Appellant, Benton Surrell Boyd, appears through counsel, Zanghi Torres Arshawsky, LLP; Respondent, John Derek Crim, appears in Pro Per. Evidence from the small claims hearing is returned to parties to use in presenting their respective cases. Parties, previously sworn, present their respective cases in the narrative, and Judge Beckman questions parties.
EXHR(A) Packet of evidence
EXHA(1) Packet of evidence
EXHA(2) Sample piece of flooring
11:30am
The Court asks Appellant's and Respondent's to each get a new estimate on refinishing the flooring, and give them to Mr. Arshawsky. Mr. Arshawasky will give the documents to Judge Beckman, who will then rule. New evidence is retained, matter is submitted.

PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar Jun-17-2009 Judgment Entered
